skill.name: pitfalls-react
skill.description: Guides React development with patterns for components, forms, error boundaries, accessibility, and responsive layouts.
skill.summary: Review React components for common pitfalls in state, forms, accessibility, and layout.
skill.author: BarisSozen

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, weak cryptography terms, key-file terms, and reconnaissance terms. Manual review found these are false positives in documentation and React example snippets, with no executable skill code, network access, filesystem access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The external command findings are Markdown fenced code examples, not Ruby or shell backtick execution. The examples show React, TypeScript, and CSS guidance only.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact locations are Markdown fence delimiters around static examples. No executable command runner or instruction to execute shell commands is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Term Match
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak cryptography hits occur in the frontmatter description text. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing logic, or encryption instruction appears in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is descriptive metadata for React guidance. It does not reference DES, MD5, SHA1, or any cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Sensitive Key File Match
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The sensitive key finding maps to a keyboard navigation example. It does not mention certificate files, private keys, tokens, or secret material.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line uses a keyboard event check for the Enter key. The surrounding section is accessibility guidance, not credential handling.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Term Match
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The system reconnaissance findings refer to UI testing, focus styles, and checklist text. No host enumeration, system probing, or command execution is requested.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are normal React and accessibility documentation. There is no semantic evidence of reconnaissance intent.

content.value_statement: React reviews often miss loading states, validation gaps, and accessibility issues. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a focused checklist for safer React component work.
actual_capabilities: Checks React components for loading, error, empty, and missing data states.
Guides form validation with Zod, React Hook Form, inline errors, and disabled submit states.
Promotes error boundaries around major React component areas.
Reviews accessibility basics such as semantic HTML, ARIA labels, keyboard support, and focus indicators.
Suggests mobile-first responsive patterns, touch target sizes, and horizontal table scrolling.
limitations: It is a guidance-only skill and does not run automated tests or linters.
It does not inspect a full application unless the user provides relevant code.
It covers common React pitfalls, not every framework-specific pattern.
It does not provide production observability or error tracking setup.

use_cases.title: Review a New Component
use_cases.description: Check a React component before merge for state handling, accessibility, and responsive behavior.

use_cases.title: Improve Form Reliability
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to identify validation, error display, and submission state gaps in a form.

use_cases.title: Audit UI Quality
use_cases.description: Apply consistent React review criteria across a feature before release.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Component Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React component for common pitfalls. Focus on loading states, error states, empty data, and basic accessibility.

prompt_templates.title: Form Validation Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React form. Check schema validation, inline errors, disabled submit states, and how invalid input is handled.

prompt_templates.title: Accessibility and Layout Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced UI review across a screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this React screen for semantic HTML, ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, focus styles, touch targets, and responsive layout issues.

prompt_templates.title: Release Readiness Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before merge or release
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the React pitfalls checklist to review this feature. Prioritize defects that could affect reliability, accessibility, or mobile users.

output_examples.input: A React dashboard component that reads query data and renders immediately.
output_examples.output: The review flags missing loading, error, and empty states. It recommends a skeleton, retryable error card, and guarded rendering.

output_examples.input: A form with client-side validation and a submit button.
output_examples.output: The review identifies missing inline error messages and submit disabling. It suggests schema-based validation and clearer user feedback.

output_examples.input: A responsive table view with icon buttons.
output_examples.output: The review checks mobile scrolling, touch target size, ARIA labels, keyboard access, and visible focus indicators.

best_practices: Provide the component, related form schema, and expected user flow together.
Ask for findings grouped by reliability, accessibility, and responsive layout.
Run normal project tests after applying any suggested code changes.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the checklist as a replacement for automated tests.
Do not add ARIA labels without checking semantic HTML first.
Do not ignore loading and error states because the happy path works.

faq.question: Does this skill change code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides review guidance and suggested improvements based on the React code you share.

faq.question: Can it review React forms?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on Zod schemas, React Hook Form usage, inline errors, and submit state handling.

faq.question: Does it cover accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks semantic HTML, ARIA labels, keyboard behavior, focus indicators, and touch targets.

faq.question: Can it replace an accessibility audit?
faq.answer: No. It helps catch common issues, but production products still need testing with real tools and users.

faq.question: Does it support responsive design reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes mobile-first breakpoints, minimum touch sizes, and scroll handling for data tables.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
